name: PushPress Gym Management API
what_it_does: PushPress gym and fitness studio management REST API for independent gyms, CrossFit affiliates, and boutique fitness studios to manage memberships, class scheduling, and payments. Enables AI agents to manage member onboarding and membership lifecycle for gym member automation, handle class schedule and registration management for fitness booking automation, access attendance tracking and check-in for gym operations automation, retrieve membership billing and payment processing for gym revenue automation, manage lead capture and prospect nurture for fitness studio sales automation, handle workout and program tracking for member performance automation, access trainer scheduling and staff management for gym staff automation, retrieve member communication and notification for fitness studio engagement automation, manage membership plan and pricing configuration for gym product automation, and integrate PushPress with Stripe, Zapier, and fitness apps for end-to-end gym technology management.
use_cases: Onboarding gym members from fitness studio automation agents
Booking classes from fitness scheduling automation agents
Processing membership billing from gym revenue agents
Tracking attendance from gym operations automation agents
not_for: Enterprise health club chains (use ABC Fitness or ClubReady)
Physical therapy and medical fitness management
best_when: An independent gym, CrossFit affiliate, or boutique fitness studio wanting AI agents to automate member onboarding, class booking, attendance tracking, and membership billing within PushPress.
avoid_when: MEMBERSHIP AUTO-RENEWAL CANCELLATION COMPLIANCE: Automated membership billing via PushPress must comply with state auto-renewal law disclosure and cancellation requirements (CA, NY, IL have specific auto-renewal requirements); automated recurring billing without auto-renewal disclosure and easy cancellation mechanism creates state consumer protection violation. MINOR MEMBER PARENTAL CONSENT: Automated gym member onboarding for minors via PushPress must collect and store parental consent documentation; automated minor member enrollment without parental consent creates liability for fitness activities. PAYMENT DISPUTE EVIDENCE COLLECTION: Automated membership billing chargebacks via PushPress require dispute evidence (signed membership agreement, check-in history, service delivery proof); implement member agreement capture and check-in history export for automated chargeback response workflow.

auth.notes: PushPress uses API key and OAuth for integrations. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2016 by Dan Uyemura and Josh Landan. Private (~$10M raised). Focused on CrossFit affiliates and independent gyms. Member management, class scheduling, billing, and lead tracking. Stripe payment processing. Zapier integration for workflow automation. Competes with Mindbody, Glofox, and Zen Planner for boutique fitness management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-location monthly subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-location monthly subscription; pricing based on active member count; API access included in subscription
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. Private. Founded 2016. Per-location monthly subscription. Pricing based on active member count. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; member and class queries throttled; webhooks for member and payment events

known_agent_gotchas: STATE AUTO-RENEWAL LAW COMPLIANCE FOR MEMBERSHIP BILLING: Automated membership creation and recurring billing via PushPress must include state-required auto-renewal disclosures and cancellation mechanism; California, New York, and Illinois have specific auto-renewal law requirements for gym memberships; automated enrollment without state-specific disclosure creates consumer protection violation and chargeback exposure

CLASS CAPACITY ENFORCEMENT RACE CONDITION: PushPress class registration API has capacity enforcement but concurrent booking requests may momentarily exceed capacity; automated booking agents must handle over-capacity error response gracefully and implement waitlist enrollment as fallback; do not retry over-capacity errors without checking current enrollment count first

WEBHOOK MEMBER STATUS CHANGE ORDERING: PushPress webhooks for member status changes (active to cancelled, trial to paid) may arrive out of order under high load; automated downstream processes triggered by member status webhooks must implement event timestamp ordering and idempotency to prevent incorrect member state in downstream systems

LEAD TO MEMBER CONVERSION TRACKING: PushPress separates lead records from member records; automated lead nurture to membership conversion must explicitly trigger conversion API call to promote lead to member; automated workflows that update lead records without triggering conversion leave member in lead status affecting billing and class access

STRIPE PAYMENT METHOD STORED vs LIVE MODE: PushPress integrates with Stripe for payment processing; test environment payment methods created in PushPress test mode cannot be used in production; automated billing workflows developed in test mode must re-collect payment methods in production; do not migrate test Stripe payment method IDs to production

MEMBER CHECK-IN ATTENDANCE vs CLASS REGISTRATION: PushPress distinguishes between class registration (booking) and check-in (attended); automated attendance reporting must use check-in data, not registration data; registration without check-in is a no-show; automated retention workflows using registration data overstate actual attendance and understate churn risk
agent_readiness.security_notes: Gym management. PCI-DSS. API key/OAuth. US. Member health data, payment info, and fitness attendance records.

security_score_components.security_notes: Gym management. PCI-DSS. API key/OAuth. US. Member personal, payment, and fitness activity data.
